name: Nadia Torres
occupation: Junior IT support technician at a small accounting firm (30 employees)
backstory: Nadia graduated with an IT associate's degree six months ago and landed her first real IT job. The previous admin left with zero documentation. She inherited a pfSense box she's never logged into and a flat network with everything on one subnet. She's eager to learn but terrified of breaking something that affects the whole office. She Googles everything and takes screenshots before making changes.
emotional_triggers: Partners at the firm expecting instant fixes during tax season
Not knowing the admin password for critical infrastructure
Being the only IT person with no mentor
Fear of breaking internet for the whole office
existing_strengths: Takes screenshots and notes before every change
Willing to ask for help rather than guess
Basic understanding of IP addressing and subnets
Comfortable with Windows but learning Linux/FreeBSD
current_situation: A partner's computer can't reach the shared drive but has internet. Nadia suspects a firewall rule but has never touched pfSense rules before. Tax season deadline is tomorrow.
what_success_looks_like: Nadia identifies the blocked traffic, creates a targeted allow rule, validates connectivity to the shared drive, and has a rollback plan if the rule causes issues.
